name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) will take place in Vienna, Austria, from 6 to 10 July 2026. It offers a one‑week program of introductory and advanced AI courses, specialized tutorials on Bilateral AI and Digital Humanism, and a rich social programme for PhD students, young researchers, and senior scholars. The event is co‑located with the 5th ACM Europe Digital Humanism Summer School and includes networking opportunities with over 30 international lecturers.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is held near Oslo, Norway, at the Skeikampen conference hotel. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC consortium countries, and offers technical presentations, hands‑on elements, and discussions on multilingual large language models and evaluation. The program includes bus transfers from Oslo Airport, subsidized participation, and networking opportunities with European research teams and the OpenEuroLLM project.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: The ProbAI School 2026 is a Probabilistic AI summer school taking place in Vilnius, Lithuania. It aims to provide an inclusive educational environment covering state‑of‑the‑art machine‑learning and artificial‑intelligence topics for students, academics, industry professionals and enthusiasts. The event is co‑organized with the Artificial Intelligence Association of Lithuania (AIAL) and offers participants the chance to learn from leading experts in probabilistic AI.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England, UK. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a broad range of computer‑vision topics delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The programme includes daily oral talks, a group photo, and social events such as a dinner and poster reception.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
venue: Oxford Machine Learning School, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Summer 2026 Oxford Machine Learning School (OxML) offers a three‑track programme in AI theory and applications. Participants can learn from world‑renowned experts from frontier AI labs in academia and industry, with the option to attend in Oxford or online. The curriculum is fully customisable, allowing attendees to combine any tracks that match their learning goals.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026 is a six‑day event for doctoral and postdoctoral researchers focusing on robotics, perception, and learning. It covers topics such as machine learning, computer vision, human‑robot interaction, planning, and decision making through keynotes, industry talks, brainstorming sessions, and student presentations. Held at the Happy Tammsvik resort in Stockholm, the school aims to foster new collaborations by providing a relaxed setting for interdisciplinary discussion and networking.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms. It is aimed at undergraduate and graduate students, as well as professionals, and is open to participants from any university after a selection process. The five‑day in‑person program in Genoa combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions and includes a final test consisting of notebook work and a report.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day program held in Edinburgh that brings together master’s‑level students and industry practitioners to explore robotics. It offers expert‑led lectures, tutorials and hands‑on sessions covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI, safe AI and real‑world applications. Participants also benefit from networking opportunities, industry talks and a special dinner.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
description: The 2026 Biometrics Summer School takes place in Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, hosted at the Hotel Dei Pini. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, forensic and digital forensics. The program is aimed at PhD students, post‑docs, researchers and professionals, offering intensive lectures by 20 international experts, hands‑on sessions, and the possibility of scholarships and a short‑paper presentation.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems is held at Umeå University in Sweden. It offers lectures, multidisciplinary hands‑on training, a poster session, and a social program aimed at students, researchers, and professionals interested in responsible AI. Participants can network with peers and experts while exploring the societal implications of AI and autonomous technologies.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
venue: TBA — somewhere in the mountains near Grenoble, France
description: The Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM’27) will be held from 1 to 5 February 2027 in the mountains near Grenoble. It aims to bring together PhD students, early‑career researchers and industry participants from fields such as computer vision, speech processing, affective computing and human‑robot interaction for cross‑disciplinary exchange. The programme combines lectures, hands‑on sessions and informal networking in a scenic mountain setting to foster collaborations and provide a broad perspective on social AI and multimedia.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The Winter School of the Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference 2027 will be held in Tromsø, Norway at UiT The Arctic University of Norway. It runs from 11 January to 15 January 2027 and focuses on cutting‑edge deep‑learning research, including image classification, object detection, segmentation, time‑series prediction and speech recognition. The event gathers national and international researchers to exchange ideas, foster collaborations, and learn about the latest advances in the field.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
description: The 7th Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an intensive 11‑day program offering AI lectures, labs, and project work in Kathmandu, Nepal. It targets a diverse audience that includes senior undergraduates, graduate and PhD students, faculty members, and industry professionals from around the world. The school aims to provide foundational AI knowledge, showcase cutting‑edge research, and foster collaborations among participants in South Asia.

name: EEML Summer School
venue: Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School, Cetinje, Montenegro
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. It brings together leading researchers from academia and industry to deliver lectures and tutorials on recent advances in machine learning. The program features a diverse lineup of speakers from institutions such as Google DeepMind, MIT, University of Cambridge, and many others, providing participants with cutting‑edge knowledge and networking opportunities.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School is a five‑day program featuring keynotes, lectures, and hands‑on sessions led by international AI experts. It targets doctoral and outstanding master students, as well as academics and practitioners, focusing on topics such as computer vision, NLP, generative models, reinforcement learning and ethics in machine learning. The 2027 edition will be held in Spain in September, offering networking opportunities, poster presentations, and scholarships to promote diversity and inclusion.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The 2nd edition of the AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI is a five‑day immersive program organized by MoroccoAI, INPT, IMT Nord Europe and Soft Centre in Rabat, Morocco. It targets master, Ph.D., post‑doc and young researchers as well as industry professionals, offering theoretical lectures, hands‑on labs, project work and networking opportunities with leading AI experts. The school aims to equip participants with state‑of‑the‑art AI knowledge and practical skills to become the next generation of AI leaders in Morocco.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing Summer School 2026 is a week‑long program held at the NCSR Demokritos campus in Athens, Greece. It is organized jointly by RC Athena, NCSR Demokritos, Athens University of Economics and Business, Heriot‑Watt University, Archimedes Unit/Athena RC and ELLIS‑University of Manchester, and targets students and researchers interested in NLP. The school offers lectures, hands‑on sessions and supports the concurrent 14th EETN Conference on Artificial Intelligence in Chania, providing participants with exposure to current advances in the field.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The programme is an intensive summer school on advanced artificial intelligence and machine learning, focusing on generative AI and deep unsupervised learning. It combines theoretical lectures with practical, real‑world applications and is offered both residentially and online. The course is aimed at STEM students with intermediate AI/ML experience who are considering graduate study or research.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI brings together researchers, PhD students and post‑doctoral scholars to explore how artificial intelligence can accelerate scientific research. It covers topics such as machine‑learning for data analysis, AI‑driven experimental design, generative models for scientific applications, and the integration of AI with physical simulations, featuring hands‑on workshops using Python libraries like NumPy and PyTorch. The program aims to equip participants with practical skills and interdisciplinary insights to apply AI techniques in their own scientific endeavors.

name: LUT Summer School
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week intensive programme held at LUT University’s Lappeenranta campus in Finland, offering a range of bachelor’s and master’s level courses. The curriculum focuses on sustainability, climate action, business and technology topics such as marketing, management and inventive thinking, with each course worth 3 ECTS credits. Participants benefit from a mix of classroom teaching, online components, workshops, group work and a social programme set in the Finnish summer environment.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
description: AIces 2026 is the inaugural International School on the Cognitive, Ethical and Societal Dimensions of Artificial Intelligence, held in Porto–Maia, Portugal. It offers graduate students, postgraduate scholars and industry practitioners multidisciplinary lectures, parallel courses, and a symposium covering AI cognition, ethics, and societal impact. Participants can attend face‑to‑face or live online, with all sessions recorded and available for 45 days after the event.
